description:
Moku is a fast, minimal manga reader frontend for Suwayomi-Server.


Moku is a fast, minimal manga reader frontend for Suwayomi-Server.

It wraps Suwayomi's GraphQL API in a lightweight Tauri app — no Electron overhead.
# Moku v0.9.1
**Released:** April 27, 2026

## New Features
- **Touch Gestures** — Pinch-to-zoom now supported in the Reader for improved touchscreen experience (#29)
- **Dual-Sync Tracking** — Automated tracking with two-way sync between the Reader and the app (#52)
- **Tracking Panel Redesign** — Revamped TrackingPanel UI (WIP)
- **Hide Completed Manga** — Completed manga are now able to be hidden in the library
- **Downloads Auto-Retry** — New auto-retry button for failed downloads, with toast notification enhancements (#51)
- **Update All Extensions** — New button to update all extensions at once
- **System-Default Theme Switching** — Automatically switch themes based on your system preference (#45)
- **Zoom Keybind Changes** — Ctrl+Scroll now used for Reader zoom (#53)

## Bug Fixes
- Fixed Download Queue priority buttons and controls not functioning properly (#54)
- Fixed Library folder tab truncation and bulk-move behavior
- Fixed Flatpak patches
- Fixed MacOS x86 detection logic (untested)

## Notes
- Linux packaging is in progress — AppImage, Flatpak, and AUR support are being actively worked on; please report any issues
- Tracking redesign is ongoing (WIP)
- Touchscreen/gesture improvements are continuing (#29, #50)
- The project repository has moved from Youwes09 to [[moku-project](https://github.com/moku-project/Moku)](https://github.com/moku-project/Moku)

**Full Changelog:** https://github.com/moku-project/Moku/compare/v0.9.0...v0.9.1
